slug='food-preservation' — lowercase kebab-case, no spaces, no uppercase.

safetyChecks.evidence: All warnings, whatNotToDo, and redFlags items have matching research findings in the research packet. Danger zone claim mapped to USDA FSIS finding. Never-taste warning mapped to FDA finding. Toxin warning mapped to USDA FSIS finding. Botulinum/mold warnings preserved from existing guide and consistent with CDC guidance. Dry ice warning mapped to USDA FSIS finding. 90F/1-hour rule mapped to USDA danger zone finding. Snow storage warning mapped to USDA FSIS finding. Refreeze rule mapped to USDA FSIS finding.
No medical or safety claims appear that are not grounded in the research packet or widely established food safety guidance.
N/A — this is a reference_guide, not an action_card. stepByStepActions is correctly empty.

Evidence tier is authoritative_only and sourceQuality is 'strong'. All 7 source references are from USDA FSIS, FDA, CDC, FoodSafety.gov, American Red Cross, and Ready.gov (FEMA) — all authoritative government/established safety organizations.

Existing guide had 2 warnings: (1) 'Improperly preserved food can harbor dangerous bacteria including botulinum toxin.' — preserved as warnings[3]. (2) 'Do not consume preserved food that smells abnormal, shows mold, or has unusual texture.' — preserved as warnings[4]. Existing redFlags also preserved: (1) 'Vomiting, diarrhea, or neurological symptoms after consuming preserved food.' — preserved as redFlags[0]. (2) 'Bloated or leaking containers, unusual smell, or unusual color of preserved food.' — split into redFlags[2] (discolored/moldy/unusual odor) and redFlags[3] (bloated/leaking containers) for clarity.

blockedByConstraints=[] — this is a general reference guide about food safety knowledge during power outages. It is not blocked by any constraint because its content (temperature rules, keep-vs-discard decisions, shelf-stable alternatives) remains relevant regardless of available resources. The guide is boosted by no_power, which is logically correct since it becomes more relevant when power is lost.

warnings: The title was changed from 'Food Preservation' to 'Food Preservation During Power Outages' to better reflect the emergency scope. The human reviewer should confirm this title change is acceptable.

food-safety-no-fridge (scenario_guide, responseRole=reference) has overlapping scope with this reference guide. The human reviewer should confirm both guides serve distinct purposes — food-safety-no-fridge covers the scenario of no refrigeration or cooking, while this guide covers the broader reference knowledge of temperature safety and keep-or-discard decisions.
recommendation: Guide passes validation. All schema checks pass and all safety checks pass. Two minor warnings for human reviewer attention: (1) title change from generic 'Food Preservation' to emergency-scoped 'Food Preservation During Power Outages', and (2) potential overlap with food-safety-no-fridge guide. Neither blocks import. Proceed to Import/Staging.
